Now I have the complete picture. Here's the full narrative:

---

# 🚙 ToughNut's 10 Days: Feb 27 – Mar 8, 2026

## At a Glance

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Total Distance** | 1,077.9 mi |
| **Total Drives** | 74 |
| **Total Driving Time** | ~25 hours |
| **Avg Efficiency** | 64% |
| **Charging Sessions** | 17 |
| **Energy Consumed** | 669.7 kWh |
| **Charging Cost** | $54.10 |
| **Est. Gas Savings** | $44.08 |
| **Phantom/Idle Range Lost** | 124.3 mi |

---

## The Narrative: A New England Winter Road Trip

This 10-day stretch tells the story of a multi-state adventure through Connecticut, Vermont, Massachusetts, and New Hampshire — anchored by a home base at **76 Popple Swamp Road, Cornwall, CT**.

---

### 📅 Day 1 — Thursday, Feb 27: The Journey North Begins
**118.4 mi · 10 drives · 62% efficiency**

The day started with short local errands around Cornwall — quick hops to Kent Road, Cream Hill Road, and Great Hollow Road. But the main event was an evening departure northward:

- **5:30 PM → 8:30 PM**: A 93.6-mile drive from Cornwall, CT to **Bennington, VT**, arriving at 28% battery. This was a significant leg through the winter countryside.
- A quick **11-minute DC fast charge at Bennington** (215 kW, $16.89) juiced up from 28% → 46%.
- Then onward: a **58-mile evening push to Ludlow, VT**, arriving around 8:50 PM at 25% battery.
- An overnight Level 2 charge in Plymouth, VT slowly brought the battery back to 53% by morning.

*Destination: ski country.* 🎿

---

### 📅 Day 2 — Friday, Feb 28: Settling into Ludlow
**70.8 mi · 4 drives · 71% efficiency**

A quiet day around the Ludlow/Okemo area. A morning drive from Plymouth to Ludlow, then a short trip up Mountain Road (likely to the ski area) and back to Dublin Road. The R1S plugged into a Level 2 charger overnight, slowly filling from 43% → 85% — gaining 139 miles of range overnight at $0 cost.

---

### 📅 Day 3 — Saturday, Mar 1: Ludlow → Back to Cornwall via Canaan
**131.1 mi · 5 drives (biggest day by trip count variety) · 62% efficiency**

Started the morning fully charged (85%) with a short drive around Ludlow. Then mid-afternoon came the big move:

- **3:30 PM → 6:00 PM**: A **123.5-mile drive from Ludlow, VT all the way down to Canaan, CT**, burning from 75% to just 17%. That's pushing the limits!
- Arrived in Canaan nearly empty and stopped at a **DC fast charger in North Canaan** (212 kW, $17.73) — charging from 14% → 34% in just 12 minutes.
- Then a scenic 17.8-mile cruise back to Cornwall at a remarkable **110% efficiency** (lots of downhill).
- An evening round trip to Kent (12.4 mi each way) before settling in.
- Plugged in overnight at home in Cornwall: 19% → 85% on the home charger ($8.62).

---

### 📅 Day 4 — Sunday, Mar 2: Cornwall → Great Barrington → Dover, VT
**160.7 mi · 8 drives · 65% efficiency**

The biggest mileage day of the whole period. Headed north again:

- Morning drive from Cornwall to **Egremont, MA** (34.3 mi) — exploring the southern Berkshires.
- Afternoon stop in **Great Barrington, MA** for errands or lunch.
- Then a **71.9-mile push from Great Barrington to Dover, VT**, arriving at 23% battery around 5:30 PM. The efficiency was 62% — cold weather and hilly Vermont roads taking their toll.
- Plugged into a Level 2 charger in Dover overnight: 23% → 85%, free of charge. Dover means one thing: **Mount Snow.** 🏔️

---

### 📅 Day 5 — Monday, Mar 3: Dover → Dummerston → West Lebanon → Stowe
**158.9 mi · 9 drives · 61% efficiency**

The R1S covered serious ground today in a northward push across Vermont:

- Quick morning moves around Dover.
- A **19-mile drive to Dummerston, VT** (near Brattleboro), then onward through Putney Landing.
- The big leg: **53.5 miles from Dummerston to West Lebanon, NH**, arriving at 33%. 
- A **23-minute DC fast charge at West Lebanon** (217 kW, free!) rocketed from 33% → 74%.
- Then the final push: **77.2 miles from West Lebanon to Stowe, VT**, arriving at 38% battery around 7:30 PM.

*Three ski areas in the rearview. Stowe is the crown jewel.* ⛷️

---

### 📅 Day 6 — Tuesday, Mar 4: Stowe → Montpelier → The Long Drive Home
**206.0 mi · 5 drives · 68% efficiency · THE BIGGEST DAY**

The longest single day of the trip and the journey home:

- Morning in Stowe (short 4.6-mi drive around town).
- **17.7 miles to Waterbury, VT** (Ben & Jerry's territory).
- **13.4 miles to Montpelier** — Vermont's capital. DC fast charged there (215 kW, free) from 18% → 47% in 14 minutes.
- **55.7 miles from Montpelier back to Lebanon, NH**. Another DC fast charge (218 kW, free): 27% → 71% in 23 minutes.
- Then the marathon: **114.5 miles from Lebanon, NH straight to Holyoke, MA**, arriving at 20%.
- Final DC fast charge at Holyoke (215 kW, free): 20% → 75% in 32 minutes, then the last **8 miles to Springfield, MA** for the night.

*Four DC fast charges in one day, three of them free. Smart routing.* ⚡

---

### 📅 Day 7 — Wednesday, Mar 5: Springfield → Home to Cornwall
**96.2 mi · 10 drives · 72% efficiency (best efficiency day!)**

The final leg of the return journey:

- **13 miles from Springfield to Windsor Locks, CT** early morning.
- **34.7 miles to Torrington, CT**, then **17.1 miles home to Cornwall**.
- Afternoon local errands: Cream Hill Road, Kent Road, school runs. Back to normal life.
- Home charging sessions brought the battery back up.

---

### 📅 Day 8 — Thursday, Mar 6: Local Day in Cornwall & Torrington
**65.7 mi · 13 drives (most drives in a single day!) · 54% efficiency (worst day)**

A busy errand day — 13 separate trips, mostly short hops:
- Early morning Cornwall → Kent Road round trip.
- **Cream Hill Road** and then **Torrington** (14.5 mi) for what looks like errands or appointments.
- Multiple stops in Torrington: Barber St, East Main St, Torringford St.
- Back to Cornwall, then out to Great Hollow Road multiple times.
- The 54% efficiency reflects all those cold starts and short trips — the R1S's least efficient day.

---

### 📅 Day 9 — Friday, Mar 7: Quiet Day
**10.0 mi · 4 drives · 44% efficiency (lowest!)**

The calmest day of the whole period. Just short local drives around Cornwall — Popple Swamp, Great Hollow, Chamberlain Road. Only 10 miles total. A single home charge added 14.5 kWh. The very low efficiency (44%) is typical of ultra-short cold-weather trips where cabin heating dominates.

---

### 📅 Day 10 — Saturday, Mar 8: Cornwall → Sharon → Litchfield
**60.2 mi · 6 drives · 61% efficiency**

A social Saturday exploring nearby Connecticut towns:
- **10.1 miles to Sharon, CT** (Hilltop Road — visiting friends?).
- Through Sharon to Dunbar Road, then a **20.3-mile drive to Litchfield, CT** at an excellent 96% efficiency.
- Bouncing around Litchfield: Whites Wood Road, South Lake Street, Doyle Road.
- **14.9 miles home to Cornwall** in the evening, arriving at just 18% battery. Time to plug in!

---

## Charging Strategy Summary

| Type | Sessions | Energy | Cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| **DC Fast (>200 kW)** | 6 | ~307 kWh | $34.62 |
| **Level 2 (home/destination)** | 11 | ~363 kWh | $19.48 |
| **Total** | **17** | **669.7 kWh** | **$54.10** |

Notable: **5 of the 6 DC fast charges were free** (West Lebanon, Montpelier, Lebanon NH, Holyoke). Only the Bennington ($16.89) and North Canaan ($17.73) sessions cost money. The overnight Level 2 charges in Vermont (Plymouth, Ludlow, Dover) were also free — likely destination chargers at lodging. Very savvy charging.

## The Story in One Sentence

**ToughNut spent 10 days on a Vermont ski trip circuit — Cornwall → Ludlow → Dover → Stowe → home via Montpelier and the Connecticut River Valley — covering 1,078 miles across five states, charging smart with mostly free DC fast chargers, and burning through a very reasonable $54 in electricity along the way.** 🏔️⚡🚙
